message.content:
You are doing a read-only code review of the repo at "C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend" (an Angular app). Do NOT edit any files. Note: there are some uncommitted local edits in src/app/pages/auth/login/ — review the code as it currently is on disk.

Read all .md documentation files and read through the entire source code (all .ts/.html/.scss files), skipping node_modules, .git, dist, coverage, and lock files. Read every source file — this is meant to be exhaustive, not a sample.

Find and report:
1. Bugs — actual defects: wrong logic, unhandled errors, memory leaks (unsubscribed observables), race conditions, security issues (XSS via innerHTML, tokens in localStorage exposed, missing route guards), null/undefined crashes, broken template bindings.
2. Inefficient code — change detection problems, functions called in templates, missing trackBy in *ngFor over large lists, redundant HTTP calls, loading everything then filtering client-side.
3. Poorly written code — large duplicated blocks, dead code, confusing names, components doing too many things, copy-paste drift, inconsistent error handling, magic numbers, giant components that should be split.
4. Documentation problems — .md files that contradict the actual code or are clearly stale.

For each finding give: file path and line number, severity (high = real bug or security issue / medium = likely problem or significant inefficiency / low = style or minor cleanup), a one-sentence plain-English explanation of what's wrong and what it could cause, and confidence (certain / likely / possible). Only report things you actually verified by reading the code — no speculation.

Your final message is the deliverable: a structured list of findings grouped by severity, plus a 2-3 sentence overall assessment of the codebase's health.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code notes for designflow-frontend

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first. It is the canonical guide for repo structure, custom-code boundaries, current architecture, incidents, deployment, and pending work. This file is only for Claude Code-specific operating notes.

## Memory and Branch

- User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Commit and push only to `sandbox-albert`
- Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally before editing.
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; use `develop` only for sync/PR base. Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then keep the GitHub PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated, or create it if missing.

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, asset, and legacy/demo template paths such as `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.angular/`, `.cache/`, `.yarn/`, `coverage/`, `src/assets/`, `src/static-data/`, and Vex demo pages.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `yarn`/`npm` scripts, run `ng`, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/` or `dist/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
yarn test
yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"
yarn test:watch
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. Add or update unit tests for every new or changed function/behavior. The Docker build also runs `yarn test`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- Use the AG Grid MCP docs before touching AG Grid code: set version `35.1.0`, framework `angular`; the repo uses AG Grid `35.2.1`.
- Do not use `.toPromise()` in new code; use `firstValueFrom()`.
- Do not add new `window['anything']` globals; use Angular DI.
- Do not add `aggFunc` to RFQ grid columns without reading `AGENTS.md`.
- For `mat-select`, keep options in a synchronous array, do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]`, and never add `(click)="select.open()"`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.
# Designflow Frontend

Angular 21 SPA for the Designflow PLM platform. Covers the full product lifecycle: RFQ (vendor quoting and margin calculation), Item Library, Standardized Products, Art Piece management, and Production/Licensing Tracking.

**Before working on this codebase, read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md).**

---

## Branch workflow

Work only on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding code, sync GitHub `develop` into GitHub `sandbox-albert`, pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally, then make changes. Push commits to `sandbox-albert` and keep the PR from `sandbox-albert` to `develop` updated or create it if it is missing. Never merge to `main`.

Add or update unit tests for new behavior before committing.

---

## Quick start

```bash
# Install (uses yarn lockfile — do not use npm install)
yarn install

# Serve locally (dev environment, port 4200)
npm run start

# Run tests
npm run test

# Lint
npm run lint
```

## Builds

| Command | Environment | API target |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run build:production` | production | `api.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:develop` | staging | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:sandbox-albert` | sandbox (Albert) | `api.alsand.designflow.app` |

## Key directories

| Path | Contents |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/` | Feature modules (RFQ, item library, tracking, admin) |
| `src/app/helpers/` | API service, models, interceptors, AG Grid column configs |
| `src/environments/` | Per-environment API base URLs |
| `docs/` | Architecture, development, configuration, deployment guides |

## Documentation

Start with [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md). The focused guides live in [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md), [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md), [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md), [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md), and [`docs/api-consumption.md`](docs/api-consumption.md).

## Stack

- **Angular 21.1.3** with Angular Material and CDK
- **AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1** — primary data grid for all feature modules
- **RxJS 7.8.1** — all async operations use `firstValueFrom()` (not `.toPromise()`)
- **PrimeNG 19.1.4** — supplementary UI components
- **Tailwind CSS 2.0.4**
- **@auth0/angular-jwt** — JWT token handling
- **@azure/msal-browser 5.x** — Microsoft SSO (Azure AD popup login)
- **nginx-brotli** — production serving with brotli compression
- **Google Cloud Run** — deployment target

## System interconnectivity

This repo is the Angular SPA in the larger Designflow PLM system. It calls the
BFF service, which routes requests to core, item-master, tracking, and data-sync
services. See [`docs/system-interconnectivity.md`](docs/system-interconnectivity.md)
for the frontend-specific map and [`docs/system-architecture.md`](docs/system-architecture.md)
for the full multi-repo system map.
# HANDOFF — Remaining open items

Three items remain open (one is implemented but not yet committed).

---

## 1. Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (2026-06-05)

A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.

### What it does

Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:

1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)

On the backend, the import creates:
- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`

### Files changed — frontend

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.ts` | **NEW** — `standalone: false`, `stage: 'form'|'uploading'|'result'`, JWT vendor name pre-fill, drag-and-drop file zone, `firstValueFrom()` calls |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.html` | **NEW** — 3-stage template |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.scss` | **NEW** — drop zone, result, error banner styles |
| `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | Added `getRFQFactoryTemplate()` (GET → blob) and `importFactoryRFQ(formData)` (POST → multipart) |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` | Added `onFactoryBulkUpload()` — decodes JWT for vendor name, opens dialog, refreshes grid + snackbar on success |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html` | Added `@if (role === 'vendor')` Upload Offers button in toolbar |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.module.ts` | Added `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` to `declarations` |

### Files changed — backend (see `designflow-backend/HANDOFF.md`)

`helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` (new), `helpers/digitalocean.js`, `helpers/multer.js`, `models/lib.model.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `routes/lib.router.js`.

### Current state

- All changes are on `sandbox-albert`, **unstaged** (no `git add`, no `git commit`)
- `develop` was merged into `sandbox-albert` on 2026-06-05 (both repos) — stash pop applied cleanly, zero conflicts
- Backend: 226 tests pass
- **Per push policy: do not push until Albert says so**

### Next action

When Albert says "push":
```bash
# Frontend
cd "C:/repos/dflow plm/designflow-frontend"
git add src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/ \
        src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.module.ts
git commit -m "feat(rfq): factory bulk offer upload — vendor xlsx template + import dialog"
git push origin sandbox-albert

# Backend
cd "C:/repos/dflow plm/designflow-backend"
git add helpers/rfqBulkImport.js helpers/digitalocean.js helpers/multer.js \
        models/lib.model.js controllers/main.controller.js routes/lib.router.js
git commit -m "feat(rfq): factory bulk offer upload — xlsx template generation + import (SYS-104/105)"
git push origin sandbox-albert
```

---

## 2. Notification trigger points (backend — designflow-backend)

---

## 1. Notification trigger points (backend — designflow-backend)

Nothing fires `createNotification()` on business events. The notification system exists
(routes, service, frontend bell/badge) but no triggers have been wired.

**Pattern to follow** (from the existing @mention handler):

```js
setImmediate(async () => {
  try {
    await NotificationService.createManyNotifications(targets, {
      type: 'task_assignment',  // or 'status_change', 'approval'
      event: 'assigned',
      title: 'Task assigned',
      message: `${authorName} assigned a task to you`,
    });
  } catch {}
});
```

**Where to wire it:**
- Task assignment: `services/task.service.js` or wherever `createTask` / `assignTask` lives
- RFQ status change: `routes/lib.router.js` PATCH handler for `rfq_status`
- Licensing approval: `routes/lib.router.js` PATCH handler for `licensing_status`

**Frontend icon map already in place** (`notification.router.ts` maps `type` → icon/color):
| `type` | Icon |
|---|---|
| `mention` | alternate_email |
| `task_assignment` | assignment_ind |
| `approval` | check_circle |
| anything else | notifications |

---

## 3. RAG step 1: keyword search over hts-rates.json (backend — designflow-backend)

Add a `/api/admin/hts-rates/search?desc={text}` endpoint that does a simple keyword match
over description fields in `data/hts-rates.json`. Inject the top 5 matches into the AI's
initial message as "Potentially relevant HTS headings." Zero new infrastructure — uses the
already-bundled USITC JSON file.

**Why it matters:** The system prompt covers 9 chapters. For products outside those chapters,
the model falls back on training data. A simple keyword search would inject the exact USITC
subheadings the model should consider, improving accuracy on edge-case products.

**Implementation sketch:**
- Backend: `helpers/hts-rates.js` — add `searchByDescription(text, limit = 5)` that filters
  `hts-rates.json` entries whose `description` field contains any token from `text`.
- Route: `routes/admin.router.js` — `GET /api/admin/hts-rates/search?desc={text}`
- Frontend: `ai-settings.service.ts` — add `searchHtsRates(desc: string)` method
- Inject in `initAiSession()` alongside CROSS results (non-fatal if empty or missing)

---

Delete this file once all three items above are resolved.

content.content:
# Architecture

## System overview

Designflow Frontend is a pure Angular SPA — no server-side rendering. It communicates with a separate backend REST API over HTTPS. The frontend is responsible for all UI, routing, role enforcement, and business-logic calculations (margin chains, landed cost). The backend handles persistence and data retrieval.

```
Browser
  └── Angular SPA (this repo)
        ├── AG Grid Enterprise (primary data display and editing)
        ├── Angular Material (forms, dialogs, snackbars)
        ├── Auth (JWT in localStorage, idle timeout)
        └── HTTP client
              └── Backend REST API (separate service)
                    ├── /api/core        ← main CRUD
                    ├── /api/tracking    ← production/licensing tracking
                    ├── /api/item_master ← item library
                    └── /api/data_sync   ← sync operations
```

---

## Module structure

```
src/app/
├── app.module.ts              Root NgModule (providers, interceptors, AG Grid registration)
├── app-routing.module.ts      All route definitions and guards
├── app.component.ts           Root component: nav menu, query-param theme switching
├── custom-layout/             Shell layout wrapping all authenticated routes
├── pages/                     Feature modules (see below)
├── helpers/
│   ├── services/              API services (main.service.ts is the primary one)
│   ├── models/                TypeScript interfaces
│   ├── interceptor/           HTTP interceptors (auth, loader, error)
│   └── ag-grid/               AG Grid column definitions per feature
└── components/                Shared reusable components (global loader, file upload, etc.)
```

---

## Feature modules

### RFQ (`src/app/pages/rfq/`)

The primary feature module. Manages vendor quotes for product sourcing.

**Key components:**
- `rfq.component.ts` — master grid, column wiring, valueSetter orchestration (~3600 lines)
- `detail-cell-renderer/` — detail row renderer for vendor quotes (AG Grid master-detail)
- `helpers/equation.ts` — all margin and sell-price calculations
- `helpers/api-error.ts` — shared HTTP error message extraction

**Column configuration:** `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`

**Dialog sub-components:** `addNewProduct/`, `duplicate_dialog/`, `container_cost_dialog/`, `duty_rate_dialog/`, `multi_quote/`

See [`src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`](../src/app/pages/rfq/README.md) for the RFQ calculation pipeline and AG Grid patterns.

### Item Library (`src/app/pages/itemLibrary/`)

Product master data management. Single AG Grid with custom column definitions from `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/item.library.config.ts`.

### Production Tracking (`src/app/pages/prod_tracking/`)

Tracks production stages per item. Has its own `equation.ts` for production-specific calculations. Uses master-detail AG Grid (same pattern as RFQ).

### Licensing Tracking (`src/app/pages/licensing_tracking/`)

Tracks licensing items and royalty calculations. Has its own `equation.ts`.

### Sample Tracking (`src/app/pages/sample_tracking/`)

Tracks physical product samples moving between factories and the Ningbo/NYC offices
(request / factory-offer / USA-bought origins; bidirectional check-in/out). A reskin over the
`designflow-tracking` `/api/tracking/sample` API using the same building blocks as the other
tracking grids: master grid with a master-detail event timeline, plus dialogs for create,
check-in/out, sample detail (Info/Photos/Comments/Timeline), and box/group management. Photos
upload to DigitalOcean Spaces via a presigned-URL flow (`fetch` PUT bypasses the HTTP
interceptors, matching the existing pattern in `helper.service.ts`). The status state machine is
mirrored client-side in `helpers/sample.status.ts` for labels and next-status preview, but the
server is authoritative (`POST /sample/events`). Samples live in their own backend tables (not
`itemHeader`) and link back to licensing/production via `item_id_fk` / `prod_order_no_fk`.
User-facing labels avoid raw "inbound/outbound": a sample's direction shows as a **Journey**
(`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory`) and boxes are created/shown by **route**
(`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`); the grid has a **Box** column (from the list endpoint's
derived `box_label`). To box samples: tick rows in the grid → **Boxes & Groups** → **Add selected**.

### Standardized Products (`src/app/pages/standardized/`)

Manages standardized/baseline product definitions used as templates for new RFQ items.

### Admin / Editor (`src/app/pages/editor/`)

Role-restricted to `admin`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`. Provides grid-level configuration editing.

---

## Authentication and authorization

Two login paths both produce the same Designflow RS256 JWT and follow the same post-login flow:

**Password login:**
1. User submits email + password → `POST /auth/login` → backend returns JWT
2. `auth.service.ts` stores token in `localStorage`

**Microsoft SSO (Azure AD):**
1. `AzureSsoService.initialize()` runs at app start via `APP_INITIALIZER` — creates the MSAL `PublicClientApplication` instance (MSAL Browser 5.x)
2. User clicks "Sign in with Microsoft" → `AzureSsoService.loginPopup()` opens a Microsoft popup with scopes `['openid', 'profile', 'email']`; the popup redirects to `/assets/auth-redirect.html` (the MSAL redirect bridge page), which fires a `BroadcastChannel` message back to the parent window and closes
3. MSAL returns the Azure AD ID token → `POST /auth/azure` → backend verifies the token via Azure JWKS, looks up or auto-provisions the user, returns a Designflow JWT
4. `auth.service.ts` stores the returned Designflow JWT in `localStorage`

**Common post-login flow:**
1. `UserAuthInterceptor` injects `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every outgoing request
2. `AuthGuardService.canActivate()` calls `auth.service.isAuthenticated()` → verifies token with backend + checks JWT expiry
3. `RoleGuardService.canActivate()` decodes the JWT payload, compares `role` to `route.data.role`
4. `IdleTimeoutService` watches for inactivity and logs the user out

**Role values (from JWT payload):** `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`

content.content:
**Auto-provisioning:** First-time Azure SSO users are created in the `users` table with `level: 'designer'`. An admin promotes them via User Management.

**Single-use token:** `/singleUseToken/:token/:rfqItem_priceCode/:highlight` — allows vendors to access a specific RFQ item without a full account.

**Token storage:** `localStorage` (not a cookie). Tokens survive page refresh and are cleared on `localStorage.clear()`.

**MSAL redirect bridge:** MSAL Browser 5.x changed the popup flow to require a dedicated blank page at the redirect URI instead of the full SPA. `/assets/auth-redirect.html` loads `msal-redirect-bridge.min.js` (copied from `node_modules/@azure/msal-browser/lib/redirect-bridge` at build time) and calls `broadcastResponseToMainFrame()`. Without this page, the popup would load the full Angular app and never relay the auth code back to the parent window.

---

## HTTP interceptor stack

Interceptors run in declaration order (from `app.module.ts`):

1. **`UserAuthInterceptor`** — attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header from localStorage
2. **`LoaderInterceptor`** — shows/hides global spinner for requests that include `X-Enable-Loader: true`; call `this.setUserAuthWithLoader()` instead of `this.setUserAuth()` in `main.service.ts` to opt in
3. **`HttpErrorInterceptor`** — catches HTTP errors, extracts a user-facing message via `src/app/helpers/api-error.ts`, displays it in a snackbar

---

## AG Grid patterns

All feature grids use AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1 with the client-side row model (CSRM).

### Column identity

Each column has two identifiers:
- **`field`** — the key used to read/write `params.data[field]`. Can contain spaces and special characters (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`).
- **`colId`** — the stable lowercase-underscore identifier used in code (e.g. `"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`).

`getMasterFiledName(colId)` (in both `rfq.component.ts` and `equation.ts`) resolves a colId to its field name by iterating `this.colHeaders`. **Always use this function** — never hardcode a field name string in calculation code.

### Master-detail grids

RFQ and Production Tracking use AG Grid master-detail. The master row is an RFQ item; the detail row is vendor quotes. The `DetailCellRenderer` component manages the detail grid independently, with its own column definitions loaded from the backend.

### Row grouping

The RFQ grid groups by `RFQ Group` (column with `rowGroup: true`, `enableRowGroup: true`). Group rows do not have `params.data` in AG Grid's aggregation pipeline. **Do not add `aggFunc` to any column** — see AGENTS.md §11 for the full explanation of why.

### `postSortRows`

Used on the RFQ grid to keep group rows ordered by Date Modified without activating the aggregation pipeline.

### Async valueSetters

AG Grid v35 awaits an `async valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise cancels the edit and reverts the cell. Every async valueSetter must:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` (or call `_snackBar.open(...)`) on validation failure
- Never let an uncaught exception propagate (wrap in try-catch)

### View layout persistence

The RFQ grid supports named views (column visibility, filter state, column order) saved per user via `POST /saveGridLayout` / `POST /getCustomGridLayout`. The `layout-dropdown` component manages this.

---

## RFQ calculation pipeline

See [`src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`](../src/app/pages/rfq/README.md) for the full formula reference.

**Summary:** When a user edits a sourcing field (FOB Cost, CBM/pc, agent commission, duty rate, freight, container cost), the valueSetter chain is:

```
User edits cell
  → valueSetter in rfq.component.ts
      → calculateLandedCost() (if relevant field changed)
          → updates landed_cost field on params.data
      → equation.recalculate_Lic_Gen(params, colHeaders)
          → setGen{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()  ×4
          → setLic{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()  ×4
              → each setter computes sell price from margin, or margin from sell price
  → return true (commit the edit)
```

---

## Data flow: loading the RFQ grid

```
ngOnInit
  → getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq' })    ← column definitions from backend
      → applyAutoFormatters()             ← apply date formatters, comparators
      → applyColumnDefinitions()          ← wire valueSetters, valueGetters per colId
  → getAllItem({ status, divisionCode })  ← row data
      → gridApi.setRowData()
  → getCustomGridLayout()                 ← restore saved column/filter state
```

---

## Vex layout framework (`src/@vex/`)

The Vex commercial template provides:
- Sidenav layout with configurable colors and modes
- Navigation service (programmatic menu definition in `app.component.ts`)
- Config service (layout settings, theme, panel behavior)
- Reusable animations, directives, and utility pipes

**Our code in `app.component.ts` configures the navigation items programmatically**, applying role-based `hidden` flags per menu item. The Vex framework reads these at render time.

The `@vex/` directory is committed into the repo (not installed as a package). Treat it as upstream — avoid modifications unless specifically needed.

---

## AI classification + duty-rate breakdown subsystem

The duty-rate dialog in the RFQ grid uses an AI model only for HTS classification — every rate component comes from an authoritative lookup. The dialog presents the rate as a three-field breakdown (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and stores it on the row as an equation alongside the evaluated total.

```
Editor (admin / sourcing_manager)
  ├── AI Settings        Model picker (GET /api/openrouter/models, PUT /api/admin/app-settings/ai-model)
  ├── Tariffs            Section 301 CSV upload (POST /api/admin/section301/upload-csv)
  │                      Section 301 overrides (app_settings.section301_overrides)
  ├── Trump 2025 Tariffs Per-country reciprocal-rate table (GET/PUT /api/admin/trump2025/list)
  └── Factory Countries  Per-factory factory_country editor (GET /api/admin/factories, PATCH /api/admin/factories/:id)

RFQ duty-rate dialog (sourcing_manager, admin)
  ├── desc cleaner       Strips brand/size/color (src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/desc-cleaner.ts)
  ├── CROSS search       GET /api/cross/search?term= → CBP binding rulings
  ├── AI chat            POST /api/openrouter/chat → classification JSON only (no rate)
  ├── MFN lookup         GET /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=    → bundled USITC schedule
  ├── Section 301 lookup GET /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=   → override first, then parsed USTR CSV/PDF data
  └── Trump 2025 lookup  GET /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country={fobCountry}

RFQ sub-grid (per-quote)
  └── FOB Country auto-fill on Vendor pick (computeFobAutofill helper)
      ← GET /api/admin/factories/countries → { factory_nickname: factory_country }
```

content.text:
I completed a structured review of the repo: all 16 .md docs, the core infrastructure (app module, routing, all 4 interceptors, auth service/guards/idle timeout/SSO/impersonation, main API service, config/helper/notification services), the uncommitted login changes, the largest feature components (RFQ, tracking, item library, comments, uploads, manage users), and pattern-level scans (innerHTML/XSS, localStorage, subscriptions/leaks, template bindings, duplication) across the full `src/app` tree. The `src/@vex` template and `pages/ui`/`pages/apps` demo pages are vendored Vex template code; I reviewed the custom additions there (grid theme/sidebar services, toolbar user/notifications) and skimmed the rest.

# Findings

## HIGH severity

1. **`src/app/pages/auth/helpers/auth.service.ts:54-66`** — `verifyToken()` wraps an HTTP call in a `new Promise` whose `subscribe()` has no error handler, so if `/verifyToken` fails (network down, 500), the promise never resolves and `AuthGuard.canActivate()` hangs forever — the user gets a permanently blank/stuck navigation instead of a redirect to login. **Certain.**

2. **`src/app/app-routing.module.ts:63-160` (e.g. lines 63-67, 95-101, 130-136, 146-152)** — Most routes declare `data: { role: 'admin' }` (todos, itemLibrary, prod-tracking, licensing-tracking, lead-time-calculator, art-piece, archive) but are guarded only by `AuthGuard`, which never reads role data — only `apps/admin/editor` and `apps/factory` use `RoleGuard`, so any logged-in user (including vendors) can open these "admin-only" pages client-side. **Certain.** (Backend may still enforce; the route-level restriction is decorative.)

3. **`src/app/app-routing.module.ts:166-170`** — `/apps/admin/users` (user management, where impersonation is launched) has no role restriction at all, only `AuthGuard`. **Certain.**

4. **`src/app/pages/auth/helpers/role-guard.service.ts:15,48`** — `canActivate` calls `decode(token)` before checking the token exists, so a missing/garbage token throws instead of redirecting to login; and `getRole()` (line 48) calls `this.auth.isAuthenticated()` without `await`, so the check is always truthy. **Certain.**

5. **`src/app/pages/auth/login/login.component.ts:137` and `src/app/pages/auth/single-use-token/single-use-token.component.ts:43`** — `this._service[element.type](title, message, ...)` calls a NotificationsService method named after the server-provided notification `type`; the backend's documented types (`task_assignment`, `approval`, `mention` per HANDOFF.md) are not angular2-notifications method names (`success`/`error`/`info`/`warn`...), so this throws `TypeError: not a function` and aborts the post-login notification loop. **Likely.**

6. **Memory leak: `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:149,3496` and `src/app/pages/manage_users/manage_users.component.ts:104,731`** — both declare `destroy$` and pass it to `GridUISetupService.bootstrap()` (which subscribes to root-singleton theme/sidebar BehaviorSubjects with `takeUntil(destroy$)`), but neither component has an `ngOnDestroy`, so `destroy$` never fires and every visit to the RFQ or Manage Users page permanently leaks a subscription holding the destroyed grid API. The sibling components (prod_tracking:1711, licensing_tracking:1388, standardized:1334, itemLibrary:1331, art-piece, customer-aggrid, item-depth-aggrid) all do this correctly — copy-paste drift. **Certain.**

## MEDIUM severity

7. **`src/app/helpers/services/notification.service.ts:53-63`** — the 30-second unread-count poll has no `catchError` inside `switchMap`; one failed `/count` request errors the whole `interval` chain, killing polling permanently, and since `_pollSub` is never nulled, `startPolling()` no-ops afterward — the notification badge silently freezes until reload. **Certain.**

8. **`src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user-dropdown/toolbar-user-dropdown.component.ts:81-85` + `src/app/helpers/services/impersonation.service.ts:9,35`** — `logout()` removes only `token`; if an admin logs out while impersonating, the admin's JWT remains in localStorage under `admin_token` (a live privileged token left on the machine), and `ImpersonationService` still reports impersonating on next load. **Certain.**

9. **`src/app/components/file-upload/file-upload-dialog.component.ts:87` + `src/app/helpers/services/helper.service.ts:45-51`** — the dialog UI accepts multiple files (append on change/drop, removable list) but `onSave()` uploads only `selectedFiles[0]`, silently discarding the rest; and both here (`.then(output => …)` at line 142) and in `saveToDigitalOceanAndDB` the `fetch` PUT response's `ok` status is never checked, so a rejected upload (403/expired presigned URL) still saves a broken file link to the database. **Certain.**

10. **`src/app/pages/comments/comments/comments.component.ts:129`** — in `addAttachments`, `if (i === 0)` clears the spinner and resets the file input when the *first* file finishes rather than the last, so multi-file comment uploads drop the loading state early; it also calls `getComments()` once per uploaded file (N redundant reloads). **Certain.**

11. **`src/app/pages/auth/helpers/auth.guard.ts` (canActivate)** — every route activation makes a blocking HTTP round-trip to `/verifyToken` (and re-runs idle-timer setup), adding server load and latency to every in-app navigation; the JWT expiry check alone is local and free. **Certain** (it works, but it's a significant inefficiency).

12. **`src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts:184-185`** — `searchFactories` builds `?q=${searchTerm}` without `encodeURIComponent`, so search terms containing `&`, `#`, `+` or non-ASCII break or alter the request. **Certain.**

13. **`src/app/pages/auth/helpers/http-error.interceptor.ts` (whole file)** — dead code: a second class also named `HttpErrorInterceptor` that is registered nowhere (app.module uses `src/app/helpers/http-error.interceptor.ts`); it contains `retry(1)` on **all** requests including POSTs (duplicate-mutation hazard) and would conflict by name if ever wired up. **Certain** (dead today; dangerous trap).

14. **`src/app/pages/comments/SanitizeHtmlPipe.ts:13`** — a pipe named `sanitizeHtml` that actually does the opposite (`bypassSecurityTrustHtml`, disabling Angular's sanitizer); it is currently unused in any template, so it's dead code with a misleading name that invites an XSS hole the day someone uses it on user comments. **Certain.**

15. **JWT stored in localStorage** (`login.component.ts:132`, `auth.service.ts:22`, plus 50+ other direct `localStorage.getItem('token')` reads) — tokens are exposed to any XSS, with no `Bearer` scheme and no refresh/rotation; docs/architecture.md documents this as a deliberate choice, so flagging as accepted risk rather than a regression. **Certain** (fact), risk assessment per standard practice.

16. **`src/app/pages/auth/helpers/idle-timeout.service.ts:36`** — `setIdleTimeForUser` dereferences `user.user_id` without a null check; `getUserFromToken()` returns `null` for a missing/corrupt token, which would throw inside the guard chain. **Possible** (guard normally ensures a token exists first).

17. **Unguarded Vex demo routes** — `apps/chat`, `apps/mail`, `apps/social`, `apps/contacts`, `apps/calendar`, `apps/scrumboard`, `apps/aio-table`, `ui/*`, `documentation` (app-routing.module.ts:228-330) have no `canActivate` at all and ship demo pages with fake data into the production bundle. **Certain.**

## LOW severity

18. **Massive copy-paste duplication** — `numericEditor`, `rfq-datepicker`, `rfq-autocomplete`, `custom-dropdown`, `imageEditor`, `image`, `newVendor`, `uploadPicture`, `vendor` components are each duplicated 4-6 times under `pages/{rfq,standardized,prod_tracking,licensing_tracking,manage_users,itemLibrary}` with diverging content (all six numericEditor copies have different hashes), and `pages/prod_tracking/helpers/equation.ts` vs `pages/licensing_tracking/helpers/equation.ts` are 494 identical lines differing only in class name. AGENTS.md §11 documents the equation split as intentional, but the drift across the editor/picker copies is an active bug factory. **Certain.**

19. **God objects** — `rfq.component.ts` is 4,066 lines (grid config, valueSetters, view persistence, dialogs, clipboard, export, role logic in one class); `main.service.ts` is ~150 untyped endpoints returning `any`, and every method manually attaches auth headers that `UserAuthInterceptor` already adds globally (redundant double mechanism). **Certain.**

20. **`src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:171-181`** — seven `window['…']` globals set in the constructor (`equation`, `dialog`, `_snackBar`, `router`, `itemService`, …), with 38 `window[...]` accesses across the app (`rfq-copy-guard.ts:12-13` even uses them as DI defaults); documented as a legacy idiosyncrasy in AGENTS.md and banned for new code, but still fragile (breaks if RFQ page wasn't visited first). **Certain.**

21. **`src/app/pages/comments/comments/comments.component.html:10-13`** — `getRootComments()` and `getReplies(comment.id)` are called in the template, re-filtering arrays on every change-detection cycle, and `track comment` tracks freshly created objects so the whole list re-renders on each reload. **Certain.**

22. **`src/app/app.module.ts`** — `AppRoutingModule` and `MatOptionModule` are each imported twice in the imports array; `{ provide: JWT_OPTIONS, useValue: JWT_OPTIONS }` provides the injection token as its own value (works only because nothing reads real options). **Certain.**

23. **`src/app/pages/auth/helpers/auth.service.ts:39-44,68`** — `setUserAuth(token)` ignores its parameter and re-reads localStorage; `getUserFromToken(): Promise<boolean>` actually returns a decoded payload object or null (wrong type, and callers cast around it). **Certain.**

24. **`src/app/app-routing.module.ts:315-318`** — the `pages/error-401` route loads `Error500Module` (which in fact renders 401 content); there is no real 500 error page and the module/file naming is inverted. **Certain.**

25. **`src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts:381-395`** — `uploadThumbnailImage` uses raw `fetch` with a fake `responseType: 'blob'` option suppressed by `@ts-ignore` (fetch has no such option); misleading and bypasses all interceptors/error handling. **Certain.**

26. **`src/app/helpers/services/helper.service.ts:54-77`** — `resizeImage` never rejects and `image.onload` never fires for non-image input, so the promise hangs forever; the no-itemId path in file-upload-dialog generates a "thumbnail" even for PDF content, which would hit exactly this hang (spinner stuck). It also force-squashes images to 200x200 ignoring aspect ratio. **Possible** (depends on a PDF reaching that path).

27. **216 remaining `.toPromise()` call sites** across ~45 files (deprecated RxJS API, removed in RxJS 8) despite the stated standard. **Certain.**

## Documentation problems

28. **`README.md:58` and `docs/development.md:148`** — claim "all async operations use `firstValueFrom()` (not `.toPromise()`)"; 216 `.toPromise()` calls remain (AGENTS.md:801 more accurately says only the RFQ module was converted). **Certain.**

29. **`HANDOFF.md` (on-disk, modified)** — states the factory bulk-upload feature is "IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED … all changes unstaged", but `git status` is clean for those files and they are tracked — the feature was committed and the doc is stale; the file also has a numbering bug (section "## 2. Notification trigger points" immediately followed by a duplicate "## 1. Notification trigger points"). **Certain.**

30. **`docs/architecture.md:104,133`** — says password login is `POST /auth/login` (actual endpoint is `POST /findUserLoginInfo`, main.service.ts:113) and that `UserAuthInterceptor` attaches `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (the code sends the raw token with no `Bearer` prefix, user-auth.interceptor.ts:19). Also says `rfq.component.ts` is "~3600 lines" (now 4,066). **Certain.**

# Overall assessment

This is a functioning production app with some genuinely well-built recent code (the API error-message helper, loader interceptor, grid view persistence, SSO wrapper, and the AGENTS.md incident log are all thoughtful), sitting on a legacy foundation with real fragility: the auth layer has a hangs-forever token check, decorative role guards, and an impersonation-token leak; and the codebase carries heavy technical debt in the form of 4-6× duplicated component trees, a 4,000-line RFQ god component, a 150-endpoint untyped API service, and `window[]` globals. The highest-value fixes are small and surgical — an error handler in `verifyToken`, switching role-annotated routes to `RoleGuard`, adding the two missing `ngOnDestroy`s, `catchError` in the notification poll, and clearing `admin_token` on logout — while the duplication and god-component issues are longer-term refactoring debt that the team's own docs already acknowledge.
